pattern: On a ring of stops the two ways around partition the total perimeter, so summing one arc and subtracting from the whole gives the other.
hints: The stops form a loop, so there are exactly two routes between any pair: one going up through the indices and one going the other way.
If you add the edge distances from the lower index up to the higher index you get one arc; the rest of the total is the other arc.
Normalize so start <= destination, sum distance[start..destination), let total be the sum of the whole array, and answer min(arc, total - arc).

edge_inputs.description: destination before start in index order
adjacent stops, short way is one edge
single edge ring of two stops

stress.description: large ring with random distances and random endpoints
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    dist = [rng.randint(0, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    start = rng.randrange(0, n)
    dest = rng.randrange(0, n)
    return [dist, start, dest]
stress.note: endpoints may coincide or be in either order
